findings.title: North Star Metric: Search Relevance Score
findings.description: The North Star metric for SearchBoost is the Search Relevance Score, which measures the effectiveness of search results as perceived by users. This metric reflects the quality of search results and is critical for user satisfaction and engagement.
assumptions: Search relevance directly correlates with user satisfaction and engagement.
recommendations: Monitor search relevance scores closely and investigate factors contributing to the drop.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Search Click-Through Rate (CTR)
findings.description: The Search Click-Through Rate (CTR) measures the percentage of users who click on search results after performing a search. This is a primary KPI that indicates user engagement with search results.
assumptions: Higher CTR indicates better relevance and user satisfaction.
recommendations: Investigate factors leading to the decline in CTR and optimize search results.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Session Abandonment Rate After Search
findings.description: The Session Abandonment Rate After Search measures the percentage of sessions where users abandon the session after performing a search. This guardrail metric must not degrade as it indicates user dissatisfaction.
assumptions: Lower abandonment rates correlate with higher user satisfaction.
recommendations: Implement measures to reduce session abandonment rates.

findings.title: Instrumentation Gap for New Query Types
findings.description: There is a significant gap in instrumentation for new query types such as image search and voice search. Without analytics events for these types, we lack visibility into their performance and user engagement.
assumptions: Instrumentation is necessary to measure the success of new features.

evidence.excerpt: No analytics events were instrumented for these new query types.

recommendations: Add baseline instrumentation for image search and voice search to enable future experimentation.

findings.title: Establish Baseline Metrics for Image Search
findings.description: Baseline metrics for image search need to be established to measure performance effectively. This includes query volume, zero-result rate, and click-through rate.
assumptions: Establishing baselines will allow for meaningful comparisons and improvements.

evidence.excerpt: We need to establish: query volume, zero-result rate, click-through rate on image results.

recommendations: Implement tracking for image search metrics to define success criteria.

summary: The analysis identifies the Search Relevance Score as the North Star metric, with supporting KPIs such as Search CTR and guardrail metrics like Session Abandonment Rate. Significant gaps in instrumentation for new query types were noted, necessitating immediate action to establish baselines for effective measurement and optimization.
